name: Reefton Spur Classic
description: The holy grail of Melbourne twisties. 95% corner ratio — 25km of non-stop switchbacks through dense rainforest. Quieter and twistier than Black Spur.

destinations.name: Marysville Country Bakery & Cafe
destinations.description: Famous meat pies and coffee. Up to 40 motorcycles parked out front on sunny weekends. The quintessential Reefton Spur finish line stop.

destinations.name: Freshly Blended
destinations.description: Quieter alternative to the bakery with garden courtyard seating. Where locals go.

destinations.name: Keppel Cafe Marysville
destinations.description: Quality coffee and food in a relaxed setting. Good outdoor seating area popular with riders.

destinations.name: Reefton Hotel
destinations.description: Historic pub licensed since 1886 at McMahons Creek. Attracts bikers in force on sunny weekends. Watch bikes roar past from the outdoor area.

warnings: Watch for gravel in wet conditions

name: Black Spur Sweep
description: Flowing fast sweepers through towering mountain ash — the tallest flowering trees on earth. Consistent-radius bends, wide road, incredible scenery.

destinations.name: Ray's Black Spur Roadhouse
destinations.description: Legendary biker fuel stop at Narbethong. Coffee, burgers, and basic supplies. A favourite on the Netrider forums.

destinations.name: Black Spur Inn
destinations.description: Top spot to warm up by the fire in winter. In summer you can't move for all the motorcyclists. Historic country pub in the forest.

destinations.name: Marysville Country Bakery & Cafe
destinations.description: Famous meat pies, big parking area for bikes. Popular biker haunt on weekends.

destinations.name: Buxton Hotel
destinations.description: Popular choice for a mid-point feed. Distinctive race bike mounted on the roof. Counter meals and cold beer.

destinations.name: Igloo Roadhouse
destinations.description: Home of the famous 'Bucky Burger'. Quick bite and coffee at 2220 Maroondah Hwy, Buxton.

warnings: Highway Patrol with laser between km markers 67-71

name: Mount Donna Buang Climb
description: Smooth, wide twisties ascending through semi-rainforest to a summit viewing tower. 70% corner ratio with a great mix of tight and sweeping bends. Light traffic.

destinations.name: Mt Donna Buang Viewing Tower
destinations.description: 21m tall tower at 1245m summit with 360-degree views over Victoria Alps and Yarra Valley. Grassy picnic area and public toilets at the top.

destinations.name: Cog Bike Cafe
destinations.description: 42 Station Road, Warburton. Coffee, cakes, and good times on the Rail Trail. Lively with cyclists and motorcyclists.

destinations.name: Warburton Waterwheel
destinations.description: Good coffee at the start/end of the ride. Last decent cafe before the climb.

warnings: Can be damp and misty at summit

name: Great Ocean Road — Torquay to Apollo Bay
description: Iconic heritage-listed coastal cruise with ocean views, cliff roads, and beach town stops. Australia's most famous motorcycle road.

destinations.name: Apollo Bay Seafood Cafe
destinations.description: Family-owned for almost two decades. Fresh local seafood, fish and chips with gummy shark (flake), mussels, oysters, local crayfish.

destinations.name: Apollo Bay Hotel
destinations.description: Pub meals, steaks, pizza. Views over the foreshore. Great for a cold beer after the ride.

destinations.name: Wye River General Store
destinations.description: 35 Great Ocean Rd, Wye River. Buzzy spot with great food and coffee. Excellent lunch stop between Lorne and Apollo Bay.

destinations.name: Grand Pacific Hotel Lorne
destinations.description: Impressive heritage hotel built 1879 at the end of Mountjoy Parade. Classic pub meals and drinks with coastal atmosphere.

destinations.name: Apollo Bay Fishermen's Co-op
destinations.description: Classic deep-fried fish and chips steps from the shore. Fresh off the boat.

name: Lorne to Deans Marsh — Otways Back Road
description: The local Reefton Spur. Climbs from the coast through Otway bush with slow-to-medium speed corners, sporadic straights for blasting, then convoluted twisting sections. Much less traffic than GOR.

destinations.name: The Store Deans Marsh
destinations.description: All-in-one hub: cafe, traditional general store, and bottle shop. Good coffee and country Anzac biscuits. 20 minutes inland from Lorne.

destinations.name: Martians Cafe & Bar
destinations.description: 12 Lorne Rd, Deans Marsh. Cafe, bar, live music venue, bottle shop and petrol station. Hippy vibe with fantastic ambience.

destinations.name: Forrest Brewing Co
destinations.description: 26 Grant St, Forrest. Sibling-owned microbrewery since 2010. Relaxed dining hall, come in your riding gear. Kid, dog, and bike friendly.

warnings: Moss on road surface in shaded sections

name: Skenes Creek to Forrest — Otway Twisties
description: Well-maintained intense twisties climbing from the coast into the Otways. Less travelled than GOR but far more technical. One tight corner after another.

destinations.name: Forrest Brewing Co
destinations.description: 26 Grant St, Forrest. Craft brewery with relaxed dining hall. Come in your riding gear. Great post-ride beers and food.

destinations.name: Forrest General Store
destinations.description: Coffee and basic supplies in the town centre. Good for a quick refuel stop.

destinations.name: Apollo Bay Seafood Cafe
destinations.description: Start or finish at Apollo Bay with fresh local seafood and fish & chips.

name: Kinglake — St Andrews Loop
description: Hairpin corners on massive cliffs along Heidelberg-Kinglake Road, phenomenal valley views, and ridge-line riding through national park forest.

destinations.name: St Andrews Hotel
destinations.description: Corner Kangaroo Ground Rd & St Andrews Rd. Known for bikes, live bands, open fireplaces, and great pub food. Motorbike and hotrod haven.

destinations.name: Kinglake General Store & Cafe
destinations.description: Coffee and basic supplies in the heart of Kinglake. Good rest stop after the climb.

destinations.name: Kinglake National Park Lookout
destinations.description: Panoramic valley views from the ridge. Pull over for photos and leg stretch.

destinations.name: Picollo Mecannico
destinations.description: Diamond Creek. Good coffee stop on the way to or from the loop. Popular with riders.

warnings: Hairpins on cliff edges with limited railing

name: Dandenong Ranges Explorer
description: Easy cruise through the Dandenongs. Great cafes, gardens, and gentle curves through charming hillside villages.

destinations.name: SkyHigh Mount Dandenong
destinations.description: Award-winning bistro at the summit with 70+ tables. Motorcycle parking $3. Coffee, lunch menu, panoramic views. Open 7 days from 10.15am.

destinations.name: Pie in the Sky
destinations.description: 43 Olinda-Monbulk Rd, Olinda. Award-winning pies since 1993. A Dandenong Ranges institution.

destinations.name: Miss Marple's Tearooms
destinations.description: 382 Mt Dandenong Tourist Rd. Purpose-built Tudor-style building with Agatha Christie theme. Scones, tea, and English fare.

destinations.name: Sassafras Bakehouse
destinations.description: Village bakery in charming Sassafras. Fresh bread, pastries, and coffee.

name: Mornington Peninsula Coastal Loop
description: Coastal cruising through wine country and seaside towns down to Cape Schanck lighthouse.

destinations.name: Flinders Hotel
destinations.description: 23 Cook Street, Flinders. Historic coastal pub since 1889. Bistro, sports bar, outdoor dining. Modern Australian cuisine, seafood, steaks.

destinations.name: Cape Schanck Lighthouse
destinations.description: 420 Cape Schanck Rd. Heritage lighthouse built 1859 on Bass Strait. Boardwalk, views, picnic area.

destinations.name: Merricks General Wine Store
destinations.description: 3460 Frankston-Flinders Rd, Merricks. Cellar door, bistro, cafe, and art gallery. Famous scones and gourmet breakfasts.

destinations.name: Arthurs Seat Eagle Summit Cafe
destinations.description: Summit cafe at the highest point on the Peninsula. Hot beverages, pies, sandwiches. Sweeping views of Port Phillip Bay.

destinations.name: The Epicurean Red Hill
destinations.description: 165 Shoreham Rd, Red Hill South. Italian bakery, cheese larder, cucina and wine centre. All locally sourced Mornington Peninsula produce.

name: Yarra Valley Full Loop
description: The ultimate Yarra Ranges day ride. Vineyards to rainforest, linking Healesville, Black Spur, Marysville, and Warburton in one big loop through Victoria's best riding roads.

destinations.name: Healesville Hotel
destinations.description: 256 Maroondah Hwy. Beautifully restored 1910 gastropub. Simple, fresh, local food. Great wine list from the Yarra Valley region.

destinations.name: Black Spur Inn
destinations.description: Country pub nestled in the mountain ash forest mid-Spur. Log fire in winter, bike-packed deck in summer.

destinations.name: Marysville Country Bakery & Cafe
destinations.description: 17 Murchison St, Marysville. Famous pies and coffee. The classic biker stop with 40+ bikes parked out front on weekends.

destinations.name: Cog Bike Cafe — Warburton
destinations.description: 42 Station Road, Warburton. Good coffee and cakes on the Rail Trail.

name: Yarra Valley Wine Run
description: Gentle rolling roads through vineyards and farmland. Perfect cruisy morning ride with great brunch stops.

destinations.name: Healesville Hotel
destinations.description: 256 Maroondah Hwy. Restored 1910 gastropub. Local wines, craft beers, and refined pub food. A warm gathering place for locals and riders.

destinations.name: Yarra Valley Chocolaterie & Ice Creamery
destinations.description: 35 Old Healesville Rd, Yarra Glen. Free chocolate tastings, Belgian-style chocolates, artisan ice cream. On a back road perfect for bikes.

destinations.name: Yarra Glen Grand Hotel
destinations.description: Historic pub in the heart of Yarra Glen. Good counter meals and cold beer with vineyard views.

destinations.name: Four Pillars Gin Distillery
destinations.description: Healesville. Award-winning gin distillery with tastings and a gin garden. Popular rider stop.

destinations.name: TarraWarra Estate
destinations.description: Winery restaurant with art gallery. Fine dining in a stunning architectural setting with vineyard views.

name: Mount Baw Baw Alpine Run
description: Serious elevation gain through alpine forest. Technical hairpin switchbacks and remote alpine scenery. Rewarding if you're up for it.

destinations.name: Noojee Hotel (Nooj Pub)
destinations.description: 411 Mt Baw Baw Tourist Rd, Noojee. Country pub on the Latrobe River. Wood-fired pizzas, gluten-free & vegan options. A favourite meeting spot for motorcyclists.

destinations.name: Mt Baw Baw Village Cafe
destinations.description: Cafe at 1460m altitude in the Alpine Village. Open Friday-Monday for meals. Recovery spot after the gruelling climb.

destinations.name: Little Red Duck Cafe
destinations.description: 1 Bennett St, Noojee. Cafe and general store in the former railway town. Good coffee and light meals.

destinations.name: Noojee Trestle Bridge
destinations.description: Historic railway trestle bridge. Short walk from the Noojee carpark. Great photo op.

warnings: Very remote — no mobile signal in parts

name: Lerderderg Gorge to Daylesford
description: Fast open roads through bush into a dramatic gorge, then on through forest to Daylesford for brunch. Under-rated western ride.

destinations.name: Farmers Arms Hotel Daylesford
destinations.description: Daylesford's oldest pub since 1857. Refined pub classics — beer battered fish, venison pot pie, duck sausages. 16 wines by the glass, 12 beers on tap.

destinations.name: Hepburn Bathhouse & Spa
destinations.description: Iconic mineral springs bathhouse. Perfect post-ride relaxation. 5 min north of Daylesford.

destinations.name: Lerderderg Gorge Lookout
destinations.description: Dramatic gorge views. Significant geological site with diverse vegetation and wildlife. Pull-off area with short walk.

name: Blackwood — Trentham Forest Loop
description: Medium-radius curves through Wombat State Forest from Bacchus Marsh, then large sweeping curves to Trentham. Cool forest riding with waterfalls and heritage towns.

destinations.name: RedBeard Historic Bakery
destinations.description: 38A High St, Trentham. Organic sourdough baked in an 1891 woodfired Scotch oven. Cafe with tours and classes. A Trentham institution.

destinations.name: Blackwood Hotel
destinations.description: 22 Martin Street, Blackwood. One of Victoria's oldest continuously licensed hotels since the 1860s. Hearty pub meals. The historic Cobb & Co stop.

destinations.name: Hotel Trentham
destinations.description: Heart of Trentham. Original pub built 1926 for local potato farmers and gold prospectors. Classic country pub atmosphere.

destinations.name: Trentham Falls
destinations.description: Victoria's tallest single-drop waterfall at 32m. Short walk from the car park. Quick scenic stop.

destinations.name: Farmers Arms Hotel Daylesford
destinations.description: Daylesford's oldest pub since 1857. Outstanding pub food with local produce. Great beer and wine selection.

warnings: Can be cold and damp in forest

name: Great Alpine Road — Bright to Omeo
description: Australia's highest year-round sealed road. Fast sweeping corners climbing to 1840m at Mount Hotham, then tight continuous twists descending to Omeo. The ultimate Victorian ride.

destinations.name: Bright Brewery
destinations.description: 121 Great Alpine Road, Bright. Award-winning craft brewery on the banks of the Ovens River. Three-time Gold Medal Winner for Best Tourism Brewery in Victoria.

destinations.name: The Blue Duck Inn
destinations.description: Omeo Highway, Anglers Rest. Legendary landmark pub for riders at a hairpin bend. About 100 motorcyclists drop in weekly. Hearty pub meals, rustic cabins.

destinations.name: Omeo High Country Cafe
destinations.description: 184 Day Ave, Omeo. Good coffee and bakery fare. Pies, cakes, and hot meals. Recovery stop at the end of the alpine descent.

destinations.name: Dinner Plain Alpine Village
destinations.description: 12km from Mt Hotham. Most riders prefer stopping here over Hotham Heights. Good cafe for food and coffee at altitude.

destinations.name: Danny's Lookout — Mt Hotham
destinations.description: Panoramic alpine views from near the summit. Iconic photo spot at 1840m. Pull-off area with short walk.

warnings: Road may be closed in snow season — check conditions before riding
Cold even in summer at altitude — pack warm layers
Long ride from Melbourne — plan overnight or early start

name: South Gippsland Coastal Cruise
description: Rolling green hills down to the coast. Easy and stunning with views all the way to Wilsons Promontory.

destinations.name: Fish Creek Hotel
destinations.description: Iconic 1939 Art Deco pub in the heart of Fish Creek. Open 7 days for lunch and dinner. Quality bistro meals. 25 minutes from Wilsons Promontory gates.

destinations.name: Loch Village Foodstore & Cafe
destinations.description: Great egg and bacon focaccia and hot chocolate. Clean toilets next door. Recommended on Netrider forums as a biker cafe.

destinations.name: Yanakie General Store
destinations.description: Last supplies before Wilsons Promontory NP. General store with petrol, basic food. Essential fuel stop — no fuel in the national park.

destinations.name: Wilsons Promontory NP Entrance
destinations.description: Entry to Victoria's most loved national park. Spectacular coastal scenery, wildlife, and walking tracks.
